quiz.title: Unit 14 Quiz 6: Polar Coordinates
quiz.subtitle: Conversions, Tangents, Areas, and Arc Length
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: Convert the polar equation $r = 2\sec\theta$ to rectangular form.
Convert the rectangular equation $x^2 + y^2 = 4x$ to polar form.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the polar curve $r = 3\sin\theta$ at $\theta = \frac{\pi}{6}$.
Find the area enclosed by one loop of the rose $r = 2\sin(3\theta)$.
questions.explanation: One loop corresponds to $0 \le \theta \le \pi/3$. Area $= \frac{1}{2}\int_0^{\pi/3} (2\sin3\theta)^2 d\theta = 2\int_0^{\pi/3} \sin^2 3\theta\,d\theta = \int_0^{\pi/3}(1-\cos6\theta)d\theta = \frac{\pi}{3}$.

questions.question: Find the points of intersection of the polar curves $r = 1+\sin\theta$ and $r = 1-\sin\theta$ for $0 \le \theta < 2\pi$.
questions.explanation: Setting $1+\sin\theta = 1-\sin\theta$ gives $\sin\theta=0 \Rightarrow \theta=0,\pi$ → points $(1,0)$ and $(1,\pi)$. The pole ($r=0$) occurs when $1+\sin\theta=0$ ($\theta=3\pi/2$) and $1-\sin\theta=0$ ($\theta=\pi/2$), so the origin is also a common point.

questions.question: Find the area of the region that lies inside the circle $r = 2$ and outside the cardioid $r = 2-2\cos\theta$.
questions.explanation: Intersection when $2 = 2-2\cos\theta \Rightarrow \cos\theta=0 \Rightarrow \theta=\pm\pi/2$. By symmetry, area $= 2\int_0^{\pi/2} \frac{1}{2}[2^2 - (2-2\cos\theta)^2] d\theta = \int_0^{\pi/2} (8\cos\theta-4\cos^2\theta)d\theta = 16-2\pi$.

questions.question: Find the length of the polar curve $r = 1+\cos\theta$ from $\theta = 0$ to $\theta = \pi$.
For the polar curve $r = 4\cos\theta$, find the points where the tangent line is vertical.
questions.explanation: $x = r\cos\theta = 4\cos^2\theta$, $dx/d\theta = -8\cos\theta\sin\theta = -4\sin2\theta$. $dx/d\theta = 0$ when $\theta=0,\pi/2,\pi,3\pi/2,\dots$. At $\theta=0$, $dy/d\theta=4\cos0=4\neq0$ → vertical tangent at $(4,0)$. At $\theta=\pi/2$, $r=0$ (the pole) and $dy/d\theta=-4\neq0$ → vertical tangent at the origin.

questions.question: Find the area of the region common to the circles $r = 2\cos\theta$ and $r = 2\sin\theta$.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the polar curve $r = \frac{1}{\theta}$ at $\theta = \pi$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered polar curves.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review area and tangent calculations.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on conversions and intersection points.
scoreMessages.0: Review polar coordinates and formulas for area and arc length.
